terms.definition: Rectified Linear Unit: activation f(x) = max(0, x), zeroing negative inputs and passing positive inputs unchanged.
Full name for ReLU—the piecewise linear activation max(0, x) used as the default nonlinearity in most deep hidden layers.
Variant f(x) = x if x > 0 else αx with small fixed α (often 0.01), allowing a non-zero gradient when pre-activations are negative.
Parametric ReLU: same piecewise form as Leaky ReLU but α is a learnable parameter, typically one value per channel or feature.
Failure mode where a ReLU neuron outputs zero for all training examples, receives zero gradient, and remains permanently inactive.
The weighted sum z = Wx + b before applying an activation function; ReLU acts on z to produce the layer output a.
